examples: Besides cellulose, there are hemicellulose and lignin.
Trees containing less lignin and more cellulose would both grow faster and also produce more ethanol.
That is easier said than done, particularly if lignin is involved, since lignin is resistant to such conversion.
And powdered lignin can be added to pulverized coal for burning in power plants.
To be fermented, cellulose must first be broken away from a plant stiffener called lignin.
